description_long: This object is a large, ancient amphitheater with a partially ruined, elliptical structure reminiscent of the Roman Colosseum. The exterior features multiple tiers of arched openings, some of which are broken or missing, revealing the interior seating and arena. The inner structure displays concentric rows of stone seating, radiating from a central performance area, with remnants of passageways and staircases visible. The facade is characterized by repetitive arches and columns, with weathered stonework and significant portions of the upper walls missing. The color palette is dominated by dark, aged stone with lighter highlights where the material is exposed. The overall impression is of a monumental, historical landmark that once hosted large public gatherings.
description: A massive, partially ruined stone amphitheater with arched facades and exposed interior seating, resembling the Roman Colosseum. The structure is elliptical, with weathered stone and significant wall damage.
description_view_0: This view highlights the open, elliptical arena and the tiered seating, with a clear look at the broken upper walls and arched exterior.
description_view_1: From this angle, the damage to the outer wall is more pronounced, and the concentric seating arrangement is visible, along with the shadowed interior.
description_view_2: This perspective shows the most intact section of the facade, with multiple rows of arches and a relatively continuous outer wall.
description_view_3: This view emphasizes the entrance area and the layered seating, with a good look at the fragmented upper structure and exposed interior corridors.
